title: Photoresistor Mapping
subtitle: light to numbers, numbers to actions!
overview: This session introduces students to Photoresistors (LDR sensors) and mapping values in Arduino. Students will learn how light intensity changes sensor values and how mapping converts sensor readings into useful outputs like LED brightness.

images._note: Add photos from actual classroom session — LDR sensor setup, Arduino circuit, Serial Monitor readings, students testing flashlight and shadow.

activity.title: LDR Sensor Light Mapping Activity
steps: Connect LDR and resistor on breadboard
Connect LDR output to Arduino analog pin A0
Open Serial Monitor to view sensor values
Cover the sensor using your hand
Observe changing values in Serial Monitor
Use map() function to control LED brightness
Test different light levels and compare outputs

quiz.q: What does an LDR sensor detect?
quiz.explanation: An LDR (Light Dependent Resistor) detects light intensity.

quiz.q: What happens to LDR resistance in bright light?
quiz.explanation: In bright light, LDR resistance becomes lower.

quiz.q: Which Arduino function reads analog sensor values?
quiz.explanation: analogRead() reads analog values from sensors like LDR.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pin is commonly used for LDR input?
quiz.explanation: LDR sensor values are usually connected to analog pins like A0.

quiz.q: What does the Serial Monitor show?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor displays sensor values and Arduino output text.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of the map() function?
options: To convert one range of values into another range
quiz.explanation: map() converts sensor values into another useful range.

quiz.q: What happens when you cover the LDR sensor?
quiz.explanation: Covering the LDR reduces light reaching the sensor.

quiz.q: Which component can be controlled using mapped values?
quiz.explanation: Mapped sensor values can control LED brightness.

quiz.q: What type of sensor is an LDR?
Which range is commonly returned by analogRead()?
quiz.explanation: Arduino analogRead() usually returns values between 0 and 1023.

quiz.q: What real-life device uses an LDR sensor?
quiz.explanation: Automatic street lights use LDR sensors to detect darkness.

quiz.q: What increases the LDR reading quickly?
quiz.explanation: More light from a torch increases sensor readings.

quiz.q: What does mapping help students understand?
quiz.explanation: Mapping helps convert one sensor value range into another useful output range.

quiz.q: Why do we use a resistor with an LDR?
options: To create a voltage divider circuit
quiz.explanation: A resistor is used with LDR to create measurable voltage changes.

quiz.q: What did students observe in this activity?
quiz.explanation: Students observed how changing light changes sensor readings and outputs.
